# results/ Directory

This directory stores **Exp 2/3 statistical analysis results** generated by
`cross_domain_analysis.py`.

The directory is intentionally empty except for this README before the final
analysis is run. Run the script from the project root:

```bash
python cross_domain_analysis.py
```

After a successful run, the expected generated output is:

```text
results/
|-- README.md
`-- cross_domain_results.json
```

## `cross_domain_results.json`

**Generated by**: `cross_domain_analysis.py` in the project root

**Contents**: All statistical metrics for Exp 2 (cross-domain comparison) and
Exp 3 (cross-method agreement) in JSON format.

### Top-level structure

| Key | Experiment | Contents |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `exp2a` | Exp 2a: Category profile | Gini coefficient + JSD |
| `exp2b` | Exp 2b: Attention entropy | Shannon entropy of receiver head scores |
| `exp2c` | Exp 2c: Causal topology | Causal reach ratio + anchor position distribution |
| `exp3` | Exp 3: Method agreement | Kendall's W / tau / top-k overlap |

### `exp2a`

```text
exp2a
|-- gini
|   |-- gpqa / math
|   |   |-- values: list[float]   # Gini coefficient per problem
|   |   |-- mean: float
|   |   `-- ci95: [lo, hi]        # Bootstrap 95% CI
|   |-- permutation_p: float      # Two-tailed permutation test p-value
|   |-- cliffs_delta: float       # Cliff's delta effect size
|   `-- effect_size: str          # negligible / small / medium / large
`-- jsd
    |-- value: float              # Jensen-Shannon Divergence, log2, range [0,1]
    |-- ci95: [lo, hi]            # Bootstrap CI
    |-- label_freq_gpqa: dict     # Frequency of 8 categories in GPQA
    `-- label_freq_math: dict     # Frequency of 8 categories in MATH
```

### `exp2b`

```text
exp2b
|-- gpqa / math
|   |-- values: list[float]       # Attention entropy per problem
|   |-- mean: float
|   `-- ci95: [lo, hi]
|-- permutation_p
|-- cliffs_delta
|-- effect_size
`-- interpretation: str           # Metric description / proxy metric note
```

Metric description: Shannon entropy is computed over each problem's
`receiver_head_score` distribution. Higher entropy means attention is spread
across more sentences; lower entropy means fewer sentences dominate.

### `exp2c`

```text
exp2c
|-- causal_reach_ratio
|   |-- gpqa / math
|   |   |-- values: list[float]   # Long-range / short-range influence ratio
|   |   |-- mean: float
|   |   `-- ci95: [lo, hi]
|   |-- permutation_p
|   |-- cliffs_delta
|   `-- effect_size
`-- anchor_location
    |-- gpqa / math
    |   |-- n_anchors: int        # Number of high-importance sentences
    |   |-- mean_pos: float       # Normalized position, 0=start and 1=end
    |   `-- values: list[float]
    |-- mann_whitney_u
    |-- mann_whitney_p
    |-- cliffs_delta
    `-- effect_size
```

### `exp3`

```text
exp3
|-- gpqa / math
|   |-- domain: str
|   |-- n_problems_used: int      # Problems with data from all three methods
|   |-- kendalls_W
|   |   |-- values: list[float]   # W per problem
|   |   |-- mean: float
|   |   `-- ci95: [lo, hi]
|   |-- pairwise_tau
|   |   `-- m1_m2 / m1_m3 / m2_m3
|   |       |-- mean: float
|   |       |-- values: list[float]
|   |       `-- ci95: [lo, hi]
|   `-- topk_agreement
|       `-- "3" / "5" / "10"
|           |-- 12 / 13 / 23: float   # Pairwise top-k set overlap
|           `-- 3way: float           # Three-way top-k overlap
`-- cross_domain_W
    |-- mann_whitney_u
    |-- mann_whitney_p
    |-- cliffs_delta
    |-- effect_size
    |-- mean_W_gpqa
    |-- mean_W_math
    `-- ci_diff_approx: [lo, hi]
```

Method mapping:

```text
m1 = Method 1, CF-KL black-box resampling
m2 = Method 2, receiver head score
m3 = Method 3, causal masking with fixed k=3 forward-window mean
```
